name: Impanadas
recipeIngredient: 1 Pound pork shoulder meat, cut into 1/2 inch cubes
1/2 Cup warm water with 1 ounce butter melted in it
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat olive oil in a 12 to 14-inch sauté pan until smoking.
2. Add onion, garlic, and fennel seeds; saute until light golden brown.
3. Add pork cubes and season with salt; cook until lightly browned.
4. Add vinegar, sugar, tomato sauce, and wine; bring to a boil.
5. Lower heat and simmer 1 hour until very tender. Remove from heat and let cool.
6. In a mixing bowl, combine cooled pork mixture, mint leaves, eggs, pecorino cheese, and parsley; stir to combine.
7. Mix flour and semolina together in a mixing bowl and make a well.
8. Add the water-butter mixture; stir to form a ball of dough.
9. Remove dough from the bowl and place on a work surface. Knead for 1 minute; wrap in plastic and refrigerate 1 hour.
10. Preheat oven to 425 degrees F (220 degrees C) and line a cookie sheet with parchment paper.
11. Roll out dough to 1/4-inch thick and cut into 6-inch circles.
12. Place two to three tablespoons of pork mixture in each circle; fold to form a half moon.
13. Seal edges by pressing with fingertips, then place on the lined baking sheet.
14. Continue until all the dough is used up.
15. Beat remaining eggs and brush each impanada with egg wash.
16. Bake for 30 to 40 minutes or until golden brown; remove from oven and serve warm with a marinated tomato salad.
Yield: 4 servings
